title: Reading to Apply a Diagram
instructions: Read the following email message about the diagram. Complete the email by filling in the blanks. Select the best choice for each blank from the drop-down menu.

sections.content:
Subject: English as a Second Language Summer Camp
To: Ray Carly <r.carly@ubz.ca>
From: Aries Singler <a.singler@ubz.ca>

Hi Ray,

Sarah and I met this morning to finalize the Summer Camp schedule but we need your input before we make it official, and inform the teachers about their __DROPDOWN_1__ Last year, a couple of teachers had to rush through the material, do you think that __DROPDOWN_2__ would resolve the problem?

You will see that this year __DROPDOWN_3__ I dedicated the afternoons for sightseeing instead. How do you feel about that?

Also, neither of us knows whether we are providing all meals or just breakfast and supper like last year. We only know that the __DROPDOWN_4__ are included in the tuition under social activities. Finally, we need to make a final decision about __DROPDOWN_5__ the campsite after the ferry ride to Victoria Island. Sarah suggested we charter a bus to pick us up at the ferry station but I thought this year's campsite was only accessible by boat.

Please let us know,
Aries

options: class times.
students' names.
schedule changes.
work benefits.
allowing students to get more sleep in the morning
giving students opportunities to socialize outside campus
making the material more appealing for teachers
starting the day at 9 a.m. instead of 9:35 a.m.
there is only one social activity per day.
all classes are concentrated before lunch.
there are no social activities in the morning.
students will not leave the campus everyday.
outings
lessons
books
accommodation
who's chaperoning the students to
the transportation to and from
how long we'll be staying at
the facilities we'll have access to at

sections.instruction_text: Using the drop-down menu, choose the best option.

options: to make a complaint.
to ask for advice.
to request changes.
to relieve him of duties.
did not take students on a camping trip.
provided students with all meals.
focused exclusively on academic activities.
featured social activities in the morning.
needs Ray to authorize the proposal before presenting it to the teachers.
created the schedule for this year's camp all by herself.
will be joining the students on the camping trip.
hasn't made any changes to the previous schedule.
